message: docs: add man pages and CI check

files.patch:
@@ -98,6 +98,21 @@ jobs:
             fi
           done
 
+  manpages:
+    name: Man Pages
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Install groff
+        run: |
+          sudo apt-get update
+          sudo apt-get install -y groff-base
+
+      - name: Generate and check man pages
+        run: scripts/check_manpages.sh
+
   rust_lint:
     name: Rust Lint (${{ matrix.os }})
     runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}

@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+.TH PT-CONFIG 5 "January 2026" "Process Triage" "File Formats"
+.SH NAME
+pt-config \- Process Triage configuration files
+.SH DESCRIPTION
+Process Triage reads configuration from:
+
+.I ~/.config/process_triage/
+
+This directory contains JSON files that control priors, policy, and learned decisions.
+.SH FILES
+.TP
+.I priors.json
+Bayesian hyperparameters. See docs/PRIORS_SCHEMA.md and docs/schemas/priors.schema.json.
+.TP
+.I policy.json
+Safety policy (protected patterns, robot mode gates). See README and docs/PLAN_GUARDRAILS.md.
+.TP
+.I decisions.json
+Learned decisions history (kill/spare). Used to bias future recommendations.
+.TP
+.I signatures.json
+User-defined signatures. See pt-signatures(5).
+.TP
+.I triage.log
+Operation log (append-only).
+.SH ENVIRONMENT
+.TP
+.B PROCESS_TRIAGE_CONFIG
+Override config directory.
+.TP
+.B XDG_CONFIG_HOME
+XDG override (default: ~/.config).
+.SH SEE ALSO
+.BR pt (1),
+.BR pt-core (1),
+.BR pt-signatures (5)

@@ -0,0 +1,111 @@
+.TH PT-CORE 1 "January 2026" "pt-core 0.1.0" "Process Triage"
+.SH NAME
+pt-core \- Process Triage inference and decision engine
+.SH SYNOPSIS
+.B pt-core
+[GLOBAL OPTIONS] <command> [ARGS]
+.SH DESCRIPTION
+.B pt-core
+scans processes, computes Bayesian posteriors, applies policy and decision theory,
+produces plans, and optionally executes actions with safety gates.
+
+No actions are taken unless explicitly requested.
+.SH GLOBAL OPTIONS
+.TP
+.BR -f , " --format" " <json|jsonl|md|summary|...>"
+Output format (default: json).
+.TP
+.BR --robot
+Enable non-interactive automation mode (still gated by policy).
+.TP
+.BR --shadow
+Run full pipeline but never execute actions.
+.TP
+.BR --dry-run
+Compute plan only (no execution even with --robot).
+.TP
+.BR --timeout " <seconds>"
+Abort if operation exceeds time limit.
+.TP
+.BR --config " <path>"
+Override config directory.
+.TP
+.BR --capabilities " <path>"
+Capabilities manifest from the wrapper.
+.SH COMMANDS
+.TP
+.B run
+Interactive golden path (scan \-> infer \-> plan \-> TUI approval \-> apply).
+.TP
+.B scan
+Quick scan only (no inference or action).
+.TP
+.B deep-scan
+Deep scan with /proc probes (Linux).
+.TP
+.B query
+Query telemetry and session history.
+.TP
+.B bundle
+Create or inspect diagnostic bundles.
+.TP
+.B report
+Generate HTML reports.
+.TP
+.B check
+Validate configuration and environment.
+.TP
+.B agent
+Agent/robot subcommands (plan, explain, apply, verify).
+.TP
+.B config
+Configuration management.
+.TP
+.B telemetry
+Telemetry management.
+.TP
+.B signature
+Signature management (list/add/remove/test/validate/export).
+.TP
+.B version
+Print version information.
+.SH EXIT CODES
+The following exit codes are stable:
+.TP
+.B 0
+OK_CLEAN (success, nothing to do)
+.TP
+.B 1
+OK_CANDIDATES (plan produced)
+.TP
+.B 2
+OK_APPLIED (actions executed)
+.TP
+.B 3
+ERR_PARTIAL (some actions failed)
+.TP
+.B 4
+ERR_BLOCKED (safety gate blocked)
+.TP
+.B 5
+ERR_GOAL_UNREACHABLE
+.TP
+.B 6
+ERR_INTERRUPTED (resumable)
+.TP
+.B 10-16
+ERR_ARGS, ERR_CAPABILITY, ERR_PERMISSION, ERR_VERSION, ERR_LOCK, ERR_SESSION, ERR_IDENTITY
+.TP
+.B 20-22
+ERR_INTERNAL, ERR_IO, ERR_TIMEOUT
+.SH FILES
+.TP
+.I ~/.config/process_triage/
+Configuration directory (policy.json, priors.json, signatures.json).
+.TP
+.I ~/.local/share/process_triage/sessions/
+Session artifacts.
+.SH SEE ALSO
+.BR pt (1),
+.BR pt-config (5),
+.BR pt-signatures (5)

@@ -0,0 +1,49 @@
+.TH PT-SIGNATURES 5 "January 2026" "Process Triage" "File Formats"
+.SH NAME
+pt-signatures \- Process Triage signature schema
+.SH DESCRIPTION
+Signatures identify known process patterns and optionally adjust priors and expectations.
+User signatures are stored in:
+
+.I ~/.config/process_triage/signatures.json
+
+Schema version: 2.
+.SH STRUCTURE
+Top-level object:
+
+- schema_version (number)
+- metadata (optional)
+- signatures (array)
+
+Signature fields (per entry):
+
+- name (string)
+- category (agent|ide|ci|orchestrator|terminal|other)
+- confidence_weight (0.0-1.0)
+- priority (integer, higher wins)
+- notes (optional)
+- patterns (object)
+- priors (optional)
+- expectations (optional)
+
+Pattern fields:
+
+- process_names (regex list)
+- arg_patterns (regex list, AND semantics)
+- environment_vars (map var -> regex)
+- working_dir_patterns (regex list)
+- socket_paths (path prefixes)
+- pid_files (path list)
+- parent_patterns (regex list)
+- min_matches (minimum pattern types)
+
+.SH CLI
+Manage signatures with:
+
+.B pt signature list|show|add|remove|test|validate|export
+
+.SH SEE ALSO
+.BR pt (1),
+.BR pt-core (1),
+.BR pt-config (5),
+.B docs/SIGNATURE_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md

@@ -0,0 +1,50 @@
+.TH PT 1 "January 2026" "pt 0.1.0" "Process Triage"
+.SH NAME
+pt \- Process Triage wrapper
+.SH SYNOPSIS
+.B pt
+[COMMAND] [OPTIONS]
+.SH DESCRIPTION
+.B pt
+is a thin Bash wrapper that locates or installs the pt-core binary and forwards all arguments.
+It provides the default interactive workflow and never performs inference itself.
+
+Safety: pt never kills processes without explicit confirmation.
+.SH COMMANDS
+.TP
+.B pt
+Run the interactive workflow (scan \-> review \-> confirm \-> apply).
+.TP
+.B pt scan
+Quick scan only (no actions).
+.TP
+.B pt deep
+Deep scan (more evidence, slower).
+.TP
+.B pt robot <subcommand>
+Agent/robot interface (plan, explain, apply).
+.TP
+.B pt update
+Update the installed pt-core via the installer script.
+.TP
+.B pt help
+Show help.
+.SH OPTIONS
+pt forwards all options to pt-core. Run:
+
+.B pt --help
+
+for the full list of global options.
+.SH FILES
+.TP
+.I ~/.config/process_triage/
+Configuration directory (policy, priors, decisions).
+.TP
+.I ~/.local/share/process_triage/
+Session data and artifacts.
+.SH EXIT CODES
+pt forwards exit codes from pt-core. See
+.B pt-core(1)
+for details.
+.SH SEE ALSO
+.BR pt-core (1)

@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@

+.TP

+.TP

+.SH ENVIRONMENT

+.BR pt-core (1),
+.BR pt-signatures (5)

@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+root_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)
+
+"$root_dir/scripts/gen_manpages.sh"
+
+# Ensure generated man pages are committed
+if ! git diff --exit-code "$root_dir/docs/man" >/dev/null; then
+  echo "Man pages are out of date. Run scripts/gen_manpages.sh and commit changes." >&2
+  git diff --stat "$root_dir/docs/man" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+# Best-effort rendering check
+if command -v groff >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  for page in "$root_dir"/docs/man/*.[15]; do
+    groff -man -Tutf8 "$page" >/dev/null
+  done
+fi

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+root_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)
+src_dir="$root_dir/docs/man/src"
+dst_dir="$root_dir/docs/man"
+
+mkdir -p "$dst_dir"
+
+for src in "$src_dir"/*.in; do
+  name=$(basename "$src" .in)
+  cp "$src" "$dst_dir/$name"
+done
+
